topic: Configure service endpoints for Azure PaaS services
key_facts: The Microsoft.AzureActiveDirectory tag listed under services supporting service endpoints is used only for supporting service endpoints to ADLS Gen 1.

For more information, see Secure Azure service access from on-premises 

 For Azure SQL, a service endpoint applies only to Azure service traffic within a virtual network's region.
A virtual network can be associated with up to 200 different subscriptions and regions by each supported service with active virtual network rules configured.

Secure Azure service access from on-premises

 By default, Azure service resources secured to virtual networks aren't reachable from on-premises networks.
To allow access to your service resources, you must allow these public IP addresses in the resource IP firewall setting.
For more information on permissions required for setting up endpoints and securing Azure services, see Provisioning .
To secure Azure service resources to a virtual network, users must have the Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnets/joinViaServiceEndpoint/action permission for the added subnets.
